{{Infobox religious building

{{nihongo|'''Himemiya Shrine'''|姫宮神社 (南伊豆町)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

== instance of (P31) ==

* {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}}

* {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}}

[[JA:姫宮神社 (南伊豆町)]]

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = Himemiya Shrine
|画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=賀茂郡}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=11|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|34|39|24.2|N|138|49|8.4|E}}}}
|所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511|qid=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}{{ill|lt=Minamiizu|Q1203741|qid=Q1203741|1=Minamiizu}} Ishiki 820
|位置   = {{coord|34|39|24.2|N|138|49|8.4|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = Iwahime-no-Mikoto
|社格   = former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}
|創建   = 
|本殿   = 
|別名   = 
|札所等 = 
|例祭   = November 2
|神事   = 
|地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Himemiya Shrine|coordinates=
{{coord2|34|39|24.2|N|138|49|8.4|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
}}
'''Himemiya Shrine''' (ひめみやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ishiki, {{ill|lt=Minamiizu|Q1203741|qid=Q1203741|1=Minamiizu}}, {{ill|lt=Kamo District, Shizuoka|Q961511|qid=Q961511|1=Kamo District, Shizuoka}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}<ref>[http://www.shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4401123 姫宮神社] - Shizuoka Prefectural Shrine Office</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It is enshrined on the northern mountain foot of the Ishiki district in the central-western part of Minamiizu.

Its founding is unknown, but it is an old shrine identified with the similarly named {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} '''Iwahime-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (いわひめのみことじんじゃ), and is also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō|Q17193087|qid=Q17193087|1=Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō}}'' as "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Iwahime no Myōjin"<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/hime2_title.htm 姫宮神社] - Genbu</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140131-01.html 姫宮神社] - Study of Engishiki Shrines</ref>. In addition, the enshrined Mishima Shrine is treated as a candidate shrine for the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} '''Takeishitsuki-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (たけいしつきのみことじんじゃ; in the ''Izu no Kuni Shinkaichō'', "Junior Fourth Rank Upper, Takeshi no Myōjin")<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was listed as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Iwahime-no-Mikoto

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}
